description: Auth-service JWT validation bug after a silent config change causes 401 storms across all downstream services. Redis shows a brief connection spike that auto-resolved (red herring).

initial_alerts.message: 503 error rate at 78% — auth-service returning 500s on all validation requests
JWT validation failure rate: 100% — NoneType error in token parser
Brief connection spike: 180 connections (normal: 40). Auto-resolved to 45.
All API calls rejected — auth middleware returning 401

initial_logs.msg: Config update applied: jwt_validation module v3.1.2
ERROR: jwt.decode() raised AttributeError: 'NoneType' has no attribute 'split'
FATAL: All JWT validation attempts failing — 100% error rate
Upstream auth-service returning HTTP 500 for all /validate calls
Auth middleware: received 401 for all user tokens — blocking requests

auth-service.msg: Deploying config update: jwt_validation module v3.1.2
Config reload complete. JWT audience field now reads from AUDIENCE_V2 env var.
ERROR: jwt.decode() — token.header.get('kid') returned None. AttributeError in split().
100% of token validations failing since config update at t=3000
Stacktrace: auth/jwt_validator.py:88 — self.audience.split(',') fails when AUDIENCE_V2 is unset

api-gateway.msg: 503 rate climbing: 78% of requests failing auth
Circuit breaker HALF-OPEN for auth-service upstream

redis.msg: Auto-scaling event: temporarily added 140 connections from batch job
Spike resolved: connections back to 45. No memory pressure.

app-service.msg: All user sessions invalidated by auth 401 responses
User-facing error: 'Authentication failed. Please try again later.'

postgres.msg: LOG: reduced query volume — app-service requests blocked by auth failure

red_herrings.reason: auto-scaling event from a batch job that completed before the incident started — connections normalised to 45 by t=2997, unrelated to auth failure at t=3000
